mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumption capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a post-recession expansion phase reflect strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, producing positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative in >55% of observations during the specified regime or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% month-over-month forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months or the predictive accuracy fails to exceed 55% in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage pressure, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter due to margin compression fears.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during a monetary tightening cycle confirms economic deceleration, leading investors to discount future earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Negative signal fails to predict downside moves with >55% accuracy during tightening regimes or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production directly correlate with improved corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations or the autocorrelation-adjusted t-statistic falls below 1.96 in out-of-sample testing.
